title: Anti-trooper
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Organization
|image=[[File:Anti-trooper_BF2.png]]
|name=Anti-trooper
|type=
|founder=[[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoan]] [[Clone Master/Legends|Clone Masters]]<ref name="BattlefrontII">''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]''</ref>
|leader=
|subgroup=
|headquarters=[[Tipoca City/Legends|Tipoca City]], [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]]<ref name="BattlefrontII" />
|locations=

'''Anti-troopers''' were [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] from the last production line based solely on the [[DNA/Legends|genetic]] [[Clone template/Legends|template]] of [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]]. Their [[Armor/Legends|armor]] and [[Blaster rifle/Legends|weapons]] dated back to the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. As such, they were equipped with [[Phase I clone trooper armor/Legends|Phase I clone trooper armor]] and the standard-issue [[DC-15A blaster rifle/Legends|DC-15A blaster rifle]]. Trained as [[Clone jet trooper/Legends|clone jet troopers]], anti-[[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] utilized [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpacks]]—granting them the advantage of height and speed in combat—in conjunction with their [[Clone trooper armor/Legends|clone trooper armor]].

This new generation of Fett [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] was brought online by a [[Kaminoan resistance|group]] of rebellious [[Clone Master/Legends|Clone Masters]], who sought to liberate their [[Homeworld/Legends|home planet]] of [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] from the rule of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. As a result, none of the anti-[[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] were loyal to [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] or [[Gender/Legends|his]] [[New Order/Legends|New Order]]—they answered only to their [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoan]] [[Scientist/Legends|creators]]. Having discovered the rogue clone [[Army/Legends|army's]] existence by [[12 BBY/Legends|12 BBY]], the Empire deployed a [[Division/Legends|division]] of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] from the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] with orders to destroy the anti-troopers and regain control of Kamino.

Composed of clone [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] who once fought for the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], the 501st invaded [[Tipoca City/Legends|Tipoca City]] where they were confronted by anti-troopers and a supplementary force of [[Rail-class Advanced Recon Commando|''Rail''-class Advanced Recon Commandos]]. Serving as the [[Legion/Legends|legion's]] mission [[Commander/Legends|commander]] was [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] and unaltered clone whom the Empire hired to lead the [[Kamino uprising|attack on Kamino]] due to his past [[Timeline of galactic history/Legends|history]] with the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] and its [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]]. Ultimately, the facility was lost to [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial forces]] as the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone War]] veterans annihilated the anti-troopers and [[Rail-class Advanced Recon Commando|''Rail''-class Advanced Recon Commandos]] as well as their masters who attempted to retreat during the fall of Tipoca City.

The creation of anti-Imperial clone troopers resulted in the reformation of the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]]. Although the Empire continued to rely on Fett clones several [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after Palpatine's [[Declaration of a New Order|accession]], the Emperor concluded that a clone army based on a single template was too susceptible to tampering. Therefore, new [[clone stormtrooper]]s were grown from different genetic templates. In time the [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]] opened its ranks to [[Reproduction/Legends|birth-born]] [[Human/Legends|Humans]] who enlisted as [[Cadet/Legends|recruits]] or conscripts. Whereas the military became increasingly diverse, only the 501st—the personal legion of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]—was permitted to keep its ranks filled solely by Fett clones.

==History==
===Origin===
{{Quote|So Palpatine has a new army. I have no doubt he'll find our cloning operations a threat one day very soon, and seek to destroy our capabilities. But he's a fool if he believes we handed over all of the combat-trained Fett clones to him.|Lama Su, Kaminoan prime minister, on discovering Palpatine's new Imperial Army|<ref name="Order66">''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]''</ref>}}
[[File:TipocaCity-CC.png|thumb|left|250px|Tipoca City, capital of Kamino and the location for the development of Anti-troopers.]]
In the final year of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], [[Prime Minister/Legends|Prime Minister]] [[Lama Su/Legends|Lama Su]] of [[Kamino/Legends|Kamino]] and [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] had a falling out; Kamino had been the prime source of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Republic army's]] [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] [[Clone trooper/Legends|troopers]] until the Chancellor commissioned new cloning facilities on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] and [[Centax-2/Legends|Centax-2]]. Utilizing [[Spaarti cloning cylinder|Spaarti technology]], new generations of Fett would be fully developed within one year as opposed to ten years. Rather than use Kaminoan scientists to oversee the new cloning centers, the Chancellor entrusted the Spaarti-grown clones to [[Arkanian Microtechnologies]]. Unknown to Chancellor Palpatine, however, Lama Su did not hand over all of the clone troopers to the Republic.<ref name="Order66" />

When the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] ended in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the Republic had been transformed into the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] was betrayed and almost completely exterminated, and Chancellor Palpatine declared himself [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|emperor of]] [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. The Republic clone troopers, programmed with absolute loyalty to the Chancellor, were re-designated as [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Imperial stormtroopers]].<ref name="SWIII">{{Film|III}}</ref> Soon afterward, Kamino found itself under the Empire's military occupation; the [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoans]] own creations had commandeered the cloning facilities to ensure that they continued to produce more [[clone stormtrooper]]s for the Empire. Disillusioned with Palpatine and resentful of the Imperial presence on their world, a [[Kaminoan resistance|splinter faction of Kaminoan]] [[Clone Master/Legends|Clone Masters]] initiated a clandestine operation to build a new clone army—one loyal to Kamino instead of the Empire.<ref name="BattlefrontII" />

Both intellectually advanced and speedily grown within the [[Tipoca City/Legends|Tipoca City Cloning Facility]], the Anti-Imperial clone troopers—otherwise known as ''Anti-troopers''—were engineered over the span of nearly a decade through Kamino's state of the art cloning technology. Like the original clone troopers that served in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the entire army of Anti-troopers were based on the [[Clone template/Legends|genetic template]] of the late [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]]. The Kaminoan Clone Masters also secretly commissioned a special forces unit of [[Rail-class Advanced Recon Commando|''Rail''-class ARC troopers]] to compliment the Anti-troopers and add to the strength of Kamino's growing army of rebel Fett clones.<ref name="BattlefrontII" />

To ensure that the Anti-troopers were superior to their older clone brothers, they were all trained as [[Clone jet trooper/Legends|jet troopers]] and were provided with [[Jetpack/Legends|limited flight jetpacks]] in addition to their [[Phase I clone trooper armor/Legends|Phase I clone trooper armor]]. They were also trained to be both expert snipers and infantry soldiers, and were thus equipped with [[DC-15x sniper rifle/Legends|DC-15x sniper rifles]] and [[DC-15A blaster rifle/Legends|DC-15A blaster rifles]], both of which were used by the original clone troopers during the Clone Wars.<ref name="BattlefrontII" />

After nearly ten years of Imperial occupation, Kamino's new clone army had reached the completion of its development and were ready to launch their "''Clone Rebellion''" in order to liberate Kamino from the Galactic Empire. Their full allegiance belonged to their Kaminoan creators, and thus they were perfectly willing to fight the Empire and kill their Imperial brethren. When the Empire discovered the Kaminoans' plot in [[12 BBY/Legends|12 BBY]], the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] was ordered to bring Kamino back into the Imperial fold by annihilating its rebel clone army. Leading the 501st was the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]], the only unique clone of Jango Fett.<ref name="BattlefrontII" />

===The Battle of Kamino===
{{Quote|Officially, there never was a 'clone rebellion' on Kamino. Unofficially, approximately twenty years after we were created, a special detachment of the Imperial 501st Legion was dispatched to Kamino with orders to eradicate an army of clones that had been bred to take arms against the Empire. Our mission commander, an expert on the inner workings of Kamino, was a young bounty hunter named Boba Fett.|Anonymous 501st stormtrooper|<ref name="BattlefrontII" />}}
The [[Kamino uprising]] commenced with Boba Fett leading the stormtroopers of the 501st Legion in an effort to infiltrate Tipoca City. Their first objective was to secure a canister that contained Jango Fett's genetic material, mainly to ensure that it was not damaged or lost during the battle. The Anti-troopers immediately engaged their Imperial brothers in combat, and though they were willing to fight to the death, they ultimately failed to prevent Fett and the 501st from getting inside of the cloning facility.<ref name="BattlefrontII" />

[[File:BF2_Kamino_5.jpg|thumb|right|250px|An anti-trooper sniper shoots Imperial [[501st Legion/Legends|501st]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] during the [[Kamino uprising]].]]

With Jango Fett's DNA secured, Boba Fett immediately returned to a secured platform where an [[Sentinel-class landing craft/Legends|Imperial shuttle]] transported the canister off-planet, thus ensuring that the Empire would have the resource required to engineer more Fett clones in the future. Meanwhile, the Anti-troopers continued to engage the 501st Legion on the city's numerous platforms. But, unable to gain an advantage or turn the tide of battle, they steadily lost more and more ground to the relentless push of the 501st stormtroopers.<ref name="BattlefrontII" />

After securing more ground on the platforms, the 501st proceeded to infiltrate the inner workings of the cloning facility with the objective of destroying the life-support system consoles that kept undeveloped rebel clones alive, thus preventing the Kaminoan rebels from cloning more Anti-troopers. Along the way, however, the Rail-class ARC troopers emerged and began attacking the 501st. But despite their best efforts, the 501st was still able to enter the city and destroy the life-support systems and abort the developing rebel clones.<ref name="BattlefrontII" />

With the battle lost and their homeworld on the verge of falling completely under the Empire's control, the Kaminoan Clone Masters attempted to retreat by utilizing a pair of [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/Legends|LAAT]] [[Gunship/Legends|gunships]] to evacuate Tipoca City. The last of the Rail-class ARC troopers joined with the remaining Anti-troopers outside of the city. Together, they made their last stand on Tipoca City's platforms in a fight to death in order to buy their creators enough time to escape. Their sacrifice was ultimately in vain, however, and the 501st was able to destroy the gunships before they took off, and thus they killed the Kaminoans who created the rebel clone army. The end of the Clone Rebellion saw every last Anti-trooper and Rail-class ARC trooper massacred down to the last clone. Tipoca City, every other Kaminoan cloning facility, and all of Kamino and its people became fully subjugated to the Galactic Empire.<ref name="BattlefrontII" />

===Aftermath===
{{Quote|The bounty hunter left after the battle was over. He said something about tracking down a smuggler on Tatooine. After the Kamino Uprising, the Emperor decided that an army of genetically identical soldiers was too susceptible to corruption. Future troopers would be cloned from a variety of templates. Though the 501st itself remained pure, the rest of the Imperial Army gradually became more and more diverse; we never really got used to the new guys.|Anonymous 501st stormtrooper|<ref name="BattlefrontII" />}}
The failure of the Anti-troopers had severe consequences not only for Kamino, but also ironically the Imperial clones of Jango Fett as well.<ref name="BattlefrontII" /> With Kamino under full Imperial rule, the Kaminoan race was forced to continue its service to the Empire by engineering countless clone stormtroopers.<ref name="TFUIIn">[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II'' novelization]]</ref> In addition to the Kaminoan scientists, Imperial [[Human/Legends|Human]] scientists were brought in to oversee the growth of Imperial clone soldiers as well.<ref name="TFUIIc">[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II (graphic novel)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II'' graphic novel]]</ref>

Although the 501st Legion proved its loyalty to the Empire once more by killing their rebel clone brothers in order to put down the Kaminoan rebellion, Emperor Palpatine ultimately decided that the ranks of the [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]] could no longer be the exclusive province of Jango Fett's progeny. Even though enlisted personnel had long since become part of the overall [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]], including the [[Imperial Army/Legends|Army]] and [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Navy]], the Fett clones took pride in the fact that they alone served as the Emperor's stormtroopers, just as they had done so for the Republic as clone troopers. The thought of serving alongside clones that were based on different templates, or even enlisted stormtroopers, was appalling to the proud clones of Jango Fett.<ref name="BattlefrontII" />

Nevertheless, their programmed loyalty to the Emperor prevented them from displaying their dismay in any way, thus forcing them to watch as the Stormtrooper Corps became increasingly diverse. Ultimately, Fett's clones were reduced to the status of a small minority and became lost in a sea of non-Fett clones and enlisted soldiers. While the 501st Legion remained pure of outsiders for a time, none of Jango Fett's clones ever became used to the idea of fighting alongside the soldiers they mockingly dubbed as "''the new guys''."<ref name="BattlefrontII" />

Roughly a decade later, the resentment only grew worse, especially for the few remaining veterans of the Clone Wars. By the year [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]], [[Clone Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Cody/Legends|Cody]] was in charge of the Imperial garrison of Kamino, most of which was composed of enlisted troopers. Having watched the obvious decline in the Imperial Army's operational effectiveness ever since the Empire increased its pool of stormtrooper recruits, Cody developed an intense hatred for all stormtroopers that were not clones of Jango Fett. He regarded them as expendable idiots with no sense of themselves or their surroundings, and would have happily traded them all for just one real clone trooper.<ref name="TFUII">''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Although the Anti troopers wear [[Phase I clone trooper armor/Legends|Phase I clone trooper armor]] in ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'', two troopers seated in the turrets of the [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/Legends|LAAT]] [[Gunship/Legends|gunships]] on the "Kamino Uprising" level are wearing [[Phase II clone trooper armor/Legends|Phase II clone trooper armor]]. After the battle's conclusion, the [[retired clone trooper]] stated that Boba Fett left Kamino, mentioning that he was going to Tatooine to track down a smuggler, obviously referring to [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]].
